Name: Thermal Protection System Overview
Description: The thermal protection system shall provide comprehensive heat shielding for all external surfaces exposed to re-entry temperatures exceeding 1260°C.

Name: Heat Shield Material Selection
Description: The TPS shall use reinforced carbon-carbon (RCC) composite tiles rated for sustained temperatures up to 1650°C on leading edges and nose cap.

Name: Tile Bonding Interface Specification
Description: Each TPS tile shall bond to the primary structure via strain-isolation pad (SIP) with a minimum peel strength of 15 kPa at operating temperature.

Name: Surface Temperature Monitoring
Description: The TPS shall include embedded thermocouple arrays at no fewer than 48 discrete locations to provide real-time surface temperature telemetry.

Name: Thermal Telemetry Data Interface
Description: Temperature sensor data shall be transmitted to the onboard data handling system via MIL-STD-1553B data bus at a minimum rate of 10 Hz per channel.

Name: Ablative Material Performance
Description: Ablative TPS materials shall demonstrate mass loss not exceeding 0.05 g/cm² per minute under peak heating conditions of 50 W/cm².

Name: Gap Filler Requirements
Description: All inter-tile gaps shall be filled with ceramic-fiber gap fillers rated to 1400°C with a maximum installed gap width of 2.5 mm.

Name: Inspection Access Provisions
Description: The TPS design shall provide non-destructive inspection (NDI) access to a minimum of 90% of bonded tile interfaces without tile removal.

Name: Thermal Cycling Endurance
Description: TPS tiles shall withstand a minimum of 100 thermal cycles from -150°C to +1260°C without degradation in structural integrity or thermal performance.

Name: Emergency Repair Kit
Description: An on-orbit TPS repair kit shall be provided capable of patching damaged tiles up to 150 cm² per repair with thermal performance equivalent to 80% of original specification.

Name: Aerodynamic Surface Tolerance
Description: Installed TPS tile surfaces shall maintain aerodynamic smoothness within ±0.5 mm of the outer mold line (OML) design contour.

Name: Lightning Strike Protection
Description: The TPS shall incorporate lightning strike protection capable of withstanding Zone 1A attachment (200 kA peak, 2×10⁶ A²s action integral) without breach of thermal barrier.

Name: Acoustic Load Resistance
Description: TPS tiles and attachment systems shall withstand acoustic loads of 165 dB OASPL during launch and ascent phases without debonding or fracture.

Name: Rain Erosion Resistance
Description: The TPS outer surface coating shall resist rain erosion at impact velocities up to 250 m/s with droplet sizes of 2 mm diameter.

Name: Thermal Protection System Weight Budget
Description: The complete TPS assembly (tiles, SIP, gap fillers, fasteners, and coatings) shall not exceed 8.5 kg/m² as an area-averaged mass.

Name: Waterproofing System
Description: The TPS shall include a waterproofing system to prevent moisture absorption exceeding 1% by weight of the tile material after 72 hours of exposure to 95% relative humidity.

Name: Structural Load Transfer
Description: TPS attachment points shall transfer aerodynamic pressure loads up to 14.4 kPa (300 psf) to the primary structure without permanent deformation of the attachment hardware.

Name: Thermal Soak-Back Limit
Description: The TPS shall limit thermal soak-back to the primary aluminum structure to a maximum of 175°C at any point during and after re-entry.

Name: Configuration Management
Description: All TPS tile locations shall be uniquely identified using a coordinate-based numbering system traceable to the vehicle coordinate reference frame.

Name: Obsolescence Management Plan
Description: The TPS design shall identify single-source materials and establish qualification of at least one alternative source for each critical material within 24 months of PDR.

Name: Inertial Navigation Unit
Description: The spacecraft shall incorporate a radiation-hardened inertial navigation unit providing 3-axis attitude determination with drift rate not exceeding 0.01°/hr.

Name: Star Tracker Integration
Description: The navigation system shall integrate dual star tracker heads providing absolute attitude determination accuracy of 5 arcseconds (3-sigma) about all three axes.

Name: GPS Receiver Specification
Description: The navigation system shall include a space-qualified GPS receiver capable of providing position accuracy of 10 m (95%) during pre-entry phases above 200 km altitude.

Name: Navigation Data Fusion
Description: The flight computer shall implement an extended Kalman filter fusing INS, star tracker, and GPS measurements with a minimum update rate of 50 Hz.

Name: Entry Interface State Vector
Description: The navigation system shall provide entry interface state vector with position accuracy of 100 m and velocity accuracy of 0.1 m/s (3-sigma) at 122 km altitude.

Name: S-Band Telemetry Downlink
Description: The communication system shall provide S-band telemetry downlink at a minimum data rate of 2 Mbps via TDRS relay during all mission phases.

Name: UHF Voice Communication
Description: The communication system shall support UHF voice communication with ground stations using TDMA protocol with a maximum latency of 250 ms one-way.

Name: Communication Blackout Mitigation
Description: The system shall implement data buffering of at least 600 seconds of telemetry to cover communication blackout periods during peak ionization.

Name: Encryption Requirements
Description: All telemetry and command links shall implement AES-256 encryption with FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated cryptographic modules.

Name: Primary Power Generation
Description: The electrical power system shall generate a minimum of 12.5 kW average power via deployable solar arrays with triple-junction GaAs cells.

Name: Battery Energy Storage
Description: The EPS shall include lithium-ion battery storage with a minimum capacity of 24 kWh providing full spacecraft power for at least 90 minutes during eclipse periods.

Name: Power Distribution Unit
Description: The power distribution unit shall provide regulated 28V±2% DC bus with overcurrent protection and autonomous fault isolation within 10 ms.

Name: Emergency Power Mode
Description: In emergency power mode, the EPS shall sustain critical avionics loads (minimum 1.5 kW) for at least 8 hours using battery reserves only.

Name: Attitude Control System
Description: The GNC system shall provide 3-axis attitude control using reaction control thrusters with a minimum pointing accuracy of 0.1° and pointing stability of 0.01°/s.

Name: Reaction Control Thruster Specification
Description: Each RCS thruster shall provide a minimum impulse bit of 0.5 N·s with a specific impulse of at least 220 seconds using hydrazine monopropellant.

Name: De-Orbit Burn Sequence
Description: The GNC system shall execute autonomous de-orbit burn sequences with delta-V accuracy of ±0.5 m/s using closed-loop thrust vector control.

Name: Entry Guidance Algorithm
Description: The entry guidance algorithm shall target landing within a 10 km radius (99.7%) of the designated landing site using bank angle modulation.

Name: Atmospheric Pressure Control
Description: The life support system shall maintain cabin pressure between 97.9 kPa and 102.7 kPa with oxygen partial pressure of 20.7-23.1 kPa.

Name: Carbon Dioxide Removal
Description: The ECLSS shall remove CO2 from cabin atmosphere maintaining concentration below 5.3 mmHg partial pressure for a crew of 4 during missions up to 14 days.

Name: Temperature and Humidity Control
Description: The thermal control system shall maintain cabin temperature between 18°C and 27°C with relative humidity between 25% and 75%.

Name: Potable Water System
Description: The water recovery system shall produce potable water meeting NASA-STD-3001 standards at a minimum rate of 2.5 L per crew member per day.
